Overview Quick start Schema architecture Interactive samples Root schema agent-collection agents example extends extensions link presentation purpose rich-text status system-info component foundation guide pattern theme token accessibility anatomy api content design-specifications document-blocks events guideline import interaction motion principle scale section state steps variant aliases category description extends last-updated links metadata preview since status summary tags thumbnail
Identifies this entity as a guide. Machine-readable identifier for the guide (e.g., 'getting-started', 'contributing', 'theming-tutorial', 'migrating-to-v2'). MUST be lowercase kebab-case and unique within the documentation group.
Pattern: ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$ Display name shown in docs (e.g., 'Getting started', 'Contributing', 'Theming tutorial', 'Migrating to v2'). Modular metadata entries. Each entry is a typed object with a kind tag. Kinds include description, status, since, last-updated, tags, category, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links. Use a category entry to classify the guide (e.g., 'getting-started', 'contribution', 'tutorial', 'concept', 'migration'). All structured docs for this guide, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Accepts the guide-specific kinds (section, steps) plus reused kinds (import for setup instructions, guideline, use-cases, accessibility, content). The kind property on each entry sets its shape. Order matters: tools SHOULD preserve it for display, since a guide reads top to bottom. Express links to other entities and external resources through a links metadata entry or a section's links, not as document block entries. AI-ready context for this guide: intent, constraints, disambiguation, anti-patterns, examples, and keywords for efficient AI use. References: entityMetadataArray, guideDocumentBlock, agents, extensions { "kind": "guide", "identifier": "getting-started", "name": "Getting started", "metadata": [ { "kind": "description", "value": "Install the Acme design system, theme your app, and render your first component." }, { "kind": "category", "value": "getting-started" }, { "kind": "status", "overall": "stable" }, { "kind": "tags", "items": [ "onboarding", "setup", "install" ] } ], "documentBlocks": [ { "kind": "section", "items": [ { "title": "Overview", "anchor": "overview", "body": "The Acme design system is a shared library of components, tokens, and patterns. This guide takes you from an empty project to a themed app with a working component in three steps." }, { "title": "Prerequisites", "anchor": "prerequisites", "body": "You need Node.js 18 or later and a React 18 project. No design tooling is required to follow this guide." } ] }, { "kind": "import", "items": [ { "platform": "react", "package": "@acme/ui", "code": "import { Button, ThemeProvider } from '@acme/ui'" } ] }, { "kind": "steps", "title": "Set up your app", "ordered": true, "items": [ { "title": "Install the package", "instruction": "Add the UI package to your project.", "examples": [ { "presentation": { "kind": "code", "language": "bash", "code": "npm install @acme/ui" } } ], "expectedResult": "`@acme/ui` appears in your `package.json` dependencies." }, { "title": "Wrap your app in the provider", "instruction": "Mount the `ThemeProvider` at the root so every component inherits the theme.", "examples": [ { "presentation": { "kind": "code", "language": "tsx", "code": "import { ThemeProvider } from '@acme/ui'\n\nexport default function App({ children }) {\n return <ThemeProvider>{children}</ThemeProvider>\n}" } } ], "expectedResult": "Components render with Acme's default light theme." }, { "title": "Render your first component", "instruction": "Drop a `Button` anywhere inside the provider.", "examples": [ { "presentation": { "kind": "code", "language": "tsx", "code": "<Button tone=\"primary\">Save changes</Button>" } } ], "expectedResult": "A themed primary button renders and responds to hover and focus." } ] }, { "kind": "guideline", "items": [ { "guidance": "Import components from the package root, not from deep internal paths.", "rationale": "Deep imports bypass the public API and break when internal file structure changes.", "level": "MUST", "category": "development" }, { "guidance": "Render every Acme component inside a single ThemeProvider.", "rationale": "Components read theme values from context; outside the provider they fall back to unstyled defaults.", "level": "MUST", "category": "development" } ] }, { "kind": "purpose", "useCases": [ { "stance": "recommended", "description": "Starting a new Acme product or prototype from scratch." }, { "stance": "discouraged", "description": "Migrating an existing app from v1 — follow the migration guide instead." } ] } ], "agents": { "intent": "Guide a developer through installing the design system and rendering a first themed component.", "constraints": [ { "rule": "Always wrap the app in ThemeProvider before using any component.", "level": "MUST" }, { "rule": "Do not import from deep internal package paths.", "level": "MUST_NOT" } ], "disambiguation": [ { "entity": "migration guide", "distinction": "Getting started is for new projects; the migration guide is for upgrading an existing app between major versions." } ], "keywords": [ "getting-started", "install", "setup", "onboarding", "provider", "first-component" ] } }

Design System Documentation Spec (DSDS) 0.6 — Draft Specification

GitHub
